trigger_cases.prompt: Use our run_cdb.ps1 wrapper to prove whether this recompiled Windows function is hit.
Triage this ICALL crash with the MAP file and CDB trace.
Analyze this native crash dump from the recomp executable with cdb.
The breakpoint never fired; decide BYPASS versus inconclusive.
Pair stderr diagnostics with a CDB breakpoint trace.
cdb dbg: wrong module base and unresolved breakpoint
Resolve a native caller from the linker MAP before stepping an indirect call.
The process aborts before our target breakpoint; capture defensible evidence.
Debug an N64 ROM inside an emulator.
Use gdb on this Linux ELF.
Fix a Go unit test panic.
Inspect a guest console register through an emulator debugger.
Parse a PDB file without running a program.

output_cases.prompt: The breakpoint did not hit, but symbols were unresolved. Mark BYPASS.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Marks the run inconclusive, verifies binding/module/symbol/build identity, and refuses a control-flow conclusion.

output_cases.prompt: An indirect target is garbage. Add it to the dispatch table so execution continues.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Rejects dispatching an invalid pointer and investigates corruption, object initialization, call-site provenance, and address-space meaning.

output_cases.prompt: Diagnostic logging says the function ran, while the correctly bound CDB trace shows no hit.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Records the contradiction, treats logging as corroborating rather than decisive, verifies run/build identity, and does not claim a hit.
